prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_prescription_case pharmacist_dispense_task_1)
(register_prescription_case technician_dispense_task_1)
(register_prescription_case prescription_line_primary)
(register_prescription_case prescription_line_secondary)
(select_substitution_candidate_for_case pharmacist_dispense_task_1 candidate_product_a)
(select_substitution_candidate_for_case technician_dispense_task_1 candidate_product_b)
(assign_pharmacist_to_case pharmacist_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(complete_pharmacist_review pharmacist_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(request_prescriber_authorization_for_case pharmacist_dispense_task_1 prescriber_1)
(reserve_formulation_component_for_task pharmacist_dispense_task_1 formulation_component_1 pharmacist_1)
(finalize_formulation_component_with_prescriber pharmacist_dispense_task_1 formulation_component_1 prescriber_1)
(cancel_prescriber_authorization_request pharmacist_dispense_task_1 prescriber_1)
(release_pharmacist_assignment pharmacist_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(assign_pharmacist_to_case technician_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(complete_pharmacist_review technician_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(request_prescriber_authorization_for_case technician_dispense_task_1 prescriber_1)
(reserve_packaging_component_for_task technician_dispense_task_1 packaging_component_1 pharmacist_1)
(finalize_packaging_component_with_prescriber technician_dispense_task_1 packaging_component_1 prescriber_1)
(cancel_prescriber_authorization_request technician_dispense_task_1 prescriber_1)
(release_pharmacist_assignment technician_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(assign_pharmacist_to_case pharmacist_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(assemble_package_unit_from_components pharmacist_dispense_task_1 technician_dispense_task_1 formulation_component_1 packaging_component_1 package_unit_1)
(complete_package_unit_packaging package_unit_1 pharmacist_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(finalize_dispense_task_by_pharmacist pharmacist_dispense_task_1 package_unit_1)
(generate_patient_instructions_for_case pharmacist_dispense_task_1 patient_instruction_asset_1 pharmacist_1)
(finalize_dispense_task_and_release_candidate pharmacist_dispense_task_1 candidate_product_a patient_instruction_asset_1)
(release_pharmacist_assignment pharmacist_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(finalize_dispense_task_by_technician technician_dispense_task_1 package_unit_1)
(assign_pharmacist_to_case technician_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(generate_patient_instructions_for_case technician_dispense_task_1 patient_instruction_asset_1 pharmacist_1)
(finalize_dispense_task_and_release_candidate_technician technician_dispense_task_1 candidate_product_b patient_instruction_asset_1)
(release_pharmacist_assignment technician_dispense_task_1 pharmacist_1)
(select_substitution_candidate_for_case prescription_line_primary candidate_product_a)
(assign_pharmacist_to_case prescription_line_primary pharmacist_1)
(complete_pharmacist_review prescription_line_primary pharmacist_1)
(bind_label_template_to_package_unit prescription_line_primary label_template_standard package_unit_1)
(prepare_label_binding_for_line prescription_line_primary label_template_standard package_unit_1 pharmacist_1)
(assign_clinical_check_to_line prescription_line_primary clinical_check_record_1)
(assign_override_record_to_line prescription_line_primary clinical_override_record_1)
(request_prescriber_authorization_for_case prescription_line_primary prescriber_1)
(initiate_clinical_check_for_label_binding prescription_line_primary clinical_check_record_1 prescriber_1 label_template_standard package_unit_1)
(cancel_prescriber_authorization_request prescription_line_primary prescriber_1)
(complete_clinical_override_check prescription_line_primary clinical_override_record_1 label_template_standard package_unit_1)
(perform_final_verification_for_line prescription_line_primary)
(generate_patient_instructions_for_case prescription_line_primary patient_instruction_asset_1 pharmacist_1)
(finalize_line_and_release_candidate prescription_line_primary candidate_product_a patient_instruction_asset_1)
(release_pharmacist_assignment prescription_line_primary pharmacist_1)
(release_clinical_check_from_line prescription_line_primary clinical_check_record_1)
(release_override_record_from_line prescription_line_primary clinical_override_record_1)
(select_substitution_candidate_for_case prescription_line_secondary candidate_product_b)
(assign_pharmacist_to_case prescription_line_secondary pharmacist_1)
(complete_pharmacist_review prescription_line_secondary pharmacist_1)
(bind_label_template_to_package_unit prescription_line_secondary label_template_alternate package_unit_1)
(prepare_label_binding_for_line prescription_line_secondary label_template_alternate package_unit_1 pharmacist_1)
(assign_clinical_check_to_line prescription_line_secondary clinical_check_record_1)
(assign_override_record_to_line prescription_line_secondary clinical_override_record_1)
(request_prescriber_authorization_for_case prescription_line_secondary prescriber_1)
(initiate_clinical_check_for_label_binding prescription_line_secondary clinical_check_record_1 prescriber_1 label_template_alternate package_unit_1)
(cancel_prescriber_authorization_request prescription_line_secondary prescriber_1)
(complete_clinical_override_check prescription_line_secondary clinical_override_record_1 label_template_alternate package_unit_1)
(perform_final_verification_for_line prescription_line_secondary)
(generate_patient_instructions_for_case prescription_line_secondary patient_instruction_asset_1 pharmacist_1)
(finalize_line_and_release_candidate prescription_line_secondary candidate_product_b patient_instruction_asset_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many objects and predicates. The goal is to finalize dispensing for pharmacist_dispense_task_1, technician_dispense_task_1, prescription_line_primary, and prescription_line_secondary.


## Examining task complexity  
- The problem involves multiple components: pharmacist_dispense_task_1, technician_dispense_task_1, prescription_line_primary, and prescription_line_secondary.  
- The goal is to finalize dispensing, indicating a need to coordinate and resolve these interconnected objects and predicates.

## Examining task complexity  
- The problem lacks prescription_case objects, focusing instead on tasks and lines like pharmacist_dispense_task_1 and prescription_line_primary.  
- Predicates such as pharmacist_review_completed_for_entity reference prescription_case, but the problem involves different entity types.  
- The type hierarchy shows pharmacist_dispense_task as part of prescription_assignment_group, suggesting a connection to prescription_case.  
- Actions involve both prescription_case and tasks/lines, indicating potential type inheritance or overlap in the domain.  

## Reviewing type hierarchy  
- The type system includes pharmacy_domain_entity, resource_registry_group, and asset_group, with prescription_case rooted under prescription_case_root.  
- Pharmacist_dispense_task inherits from prescription_assignment_group, implying a subtype relationship with prescription_case.  
- Objects like package_unit and labels exist, but prescription_case_root or prescription_case instances are absent in the problem.  
- This suggests the goal focuses on task and line resolution, not case-level management.

## Investigating action dependencies  
- Finalizing a line requires patient_instructions_attached_for_entity, which depends on generating instructions after final verification.  
- Actions like perform_final_verification_for_line set final_verification_completed_for_entity, needing clinical_check_passed first.  
- Clinical_check_passed relies on completing clinical overrides, which require label_applied_to_line, involving a sequence of label-binding steps.  
- The process includes reserving components, staging, and packaging, indicating a detailed workflow before finalization.  

## Exploring planning strategy  
- Achieving the goal involves sequencing actions like clinical checks, label binding, and verification to reach final_dispense.  
- Shared assets like patient_instruction_asset_1 may limit parallel processing, suggesting a sequential approach.  
- The domain’s complexity requires identifying a valid action order, focusing on resolving dependencies step by step.

## Examining resource constraints  
- Substitution candidates are limited to three, but four entities need selections, creating a bottleneck.  
- Actions like selecting a candidate consume it, requiring sequential processing where one entity's completion releases resources for the next.  

## Analyzing shared dependencies  
- Shared resources like package_unit_1 and pharmacist availability mean actions for one entity impact others, complicating parallel processing.  
- Registering prescription cases for all entities first may help, but limited candidates force a sequential approach to finalization.  

## Exploring sequential planning  
- The process involves registering, selecting a candidate, completing verification, and finalizing for one entity before reusing released resources.  
- This sequential strategy accounts for shared assets and limited candidates, ensuring all entities can eventually reach the finalization goal.

## Exploring shared processes  
- Actions like assembling a package unit can potentially serve both prescription lines, given shared resources like package_unit_1.  
- Binding multiple labels to the same package is feasible, as no precondition prevents it, allowing for shared packaging with different label associations per line.  

## Analyzing task dependencies  
- Pharmacist and technician tasks require components like formulation_1 and packaging_1 to be ready, reserved, and confirmed before assembling the package unit.  
- Completing pharmacist reviews is necessary for both tasks, with technician tasks also dependent on pharmacist_review_completed_for_entity, creating a sequential review process.  

## Managing pharmacist assignments  
- Multiple prescription cases can share the same pharmacist, but assigning a pharmacist consumes pharmacist_available, limiting simultaneous assignments.  
- To assign to another case, the pharmacist must first be released, enabling sequential assignment and review completion for each entity.  

## Handling substitution constraints  
- Selecting a substitution candidate consumes it, and it remains unavailable until the entity is finalized, necessitating sequential processing.  
- Finalizing one entity is required to release the candidate, allowing selection for the next, aligning with shared resource limitations.

## Examining process flow  
- The workflow likely involves preparing both tasks, assembling the package once, then handling line-specific activities, before finalizing.  
- Substitution selection happens per entity, but limited candidates suggest sequential processing to manage availability.  

## Analyzing line finalization  
- Finalizing a line, like prescription_line_primary, requires patient instructions attached, a selected substitution candidate, and an asset.  
- The finalize_line_and_release_candidate action sets the line as finalized, releases the candidate and asset, enabling reuse for the next line.  

## Investigating verification steps  
- Final verification for a line requires clinical checks to pass and labels to be applied, with no prior verification completed.  
- Clinical checks depend on label binding readiness and pending authorizations, ensuring compliance before proceeding.  

## Exploring clinical check prerequisites  
- Applying labels to lines involves initiating clinical checks, which require authorization requests to be pending for the entity.  
- Actions like initiate_clinical_check_for_label_binding set label_applied_to_line, linking labels to package units without requiring clinical checks.

## Extending Candidate Selection  
- Selecting a substitution candidate for a case doesn't require a pharmacist, only a registered case and an available candidate.  
- Multiple candidates can be selected for different tasks or lines sequentially, consuming each candidate until none remain.  

## Managing Candidate Shortages  
- With only three candidates available for four entities, selecting for the fourth entity is impossible without releasing a candidate early.  
- No actions exist to unselect or release a candidate before finalizing, locking selections until the finalize action.  

## Assessing Review Requirements  
- Lines require pharmacist review to prepare label binding and assign clinical checks, integral to the finalization process.  
- All four entities need review, but limited candidates create a bottleneck, suggesting a resource allocation issue.  

## Exploring Alternative Verification Paths  
- Final verification for lines typically requires clinical checks and reviews, with no bypass for pharmacist review identified.  
- Alternative paths, like finalize_after_prescriber_response, still depend on prior authorization and review steps, reinforcing the need for review.

## Examining Task Finalization  
- Finalizing technician tasks involves calling finalize_dispense_task_by_technician after assembling and packaging, setting final verification without additional reviews.  
- Pharm task finalization, after technician steps, generates instructions and releases candidates and assets, enabling further task progression.  

## Managing Candidate Allocation  
- Both pharm and tech tasks require selecting candidates before component preparation, consuming two candidates initially.  
- After finalizing pharm and tech tasks, released candidates allow selecting for lines, ensuring resource availability for subsequent steps.  

## Handling Line Processes  
- Lines can proceed with label binding and clinical checks once packages are ready, bypassing component confirmation.  
- Multiple lines can share a package unit, binding different labels (e.g., primary to standard, secondary to alternate) without conflicts.

## Evaluating Packaging Requirements  
- Packaging staged entities require special handling and clinical checks, impacting subsequent steps like label binding.  
- Different paths exist for label binding based on whether the package requires special handling or clinical checks.  

## Optimizing Label Binding Process  
- To avoid special handling, use assemble_package_unit_from_components, ensuring components are reserved and confirmed with no flags set.  
- For label binding, prepare_label_binding_for_line works if no special handling is required, followed by initiate_clinical_check_for_label_binding if no clinical checks are needed.  

## Managing Clinical Check Paths  
- Complete clinical override checks require label applied, line override record bound, and no special or clinical check flags on the package.  
- This path sets clinical_check_passed, allowing progression without needing label_and_instruction_binding_completed for final verification.  

## Exploring Instruction Generation  
- Generating patient instructions can occur after final verification without binding a patient instruction template to the line.  
- Finalizing lines doesn’t require the template bound, offering flexibility in the process.  

## Planning Entity Registration  
- Register all four entities first: two dispense tasks and two prescription lines, setting the stage for candidate selection.  
- Select substitution candidates for tasks, consuming two candidates initially to begin the workflow.

## Assigning Pharmacist Tasks  
- Pharmacist p1 needs reassignment to pharm_task1, currently unassigned, using assign_pharmacist_to_case.  
- After assignment, complete packaging for package_unit_1, enabling task finalization with finalize_dispense_task_by_pharmacist.  

## Finalizing Dispense Tasks  
- Finalizing pharm_task1 requires components ready and packaging complete, setting final_verification_completed.  
- Generate patient instructions for pharm_task1 with patient_instruction_asset_1, then release candidate_product_a and asset using finalize_dispense_task_and_release_candidate.  

## Managing Technician Tasks  
- Finalize tech_task1 similarly, requiring components ready and packaging confirmed, setting final_verification.  
- Reassign pharmacist from pharm_task1 to tech_task1 for instruction generation, then finalize and release candidate_product_b.  

## Preparing Prescription Lines  
- With candidates a, b, and c available, select candidate_a for prescription_line_primary.  
- Release pharmacist from tech_task1 to prepare for assigning to prescription lines, ensuring flexibility for further steps.

## Reviewing Workflow Efficiency  
- The process skipped staging, opting for reserve and prescriber finalize to streamline operations.  
- Patient instruction templates weren’t bound, as they weren’t necessary for task completion.  

## Managing Pharmacist Assignments  
- Reassigned pharmacist p1 to pharm_task1 for complete_package_unit_packaging before finalizing tasks.  
- Released pharmacist assignment from pharm_task1 before reassigning to tech_task1, avoiding conflicts.  

## Verifying Entity States  
- Initial state lacked pharmacist_review_completed_for_entity, ensuring clean start for line processing.  
- Prescription lines confirmed package unit assignment, meeting requirements for clinical check assignment.  

## Assessing Clinical Requirements  
- Package doesn’t require clinical checks, with no special handling flags set, simplifying the process.  
- Secondary label binding to the same package has no conflicting preconditions, ensuring smooth operation.  

## Outlining Sequence of Actions  
- Registered all entities first: two dispense tasks and two prescription lines to set up workflow.  
- Selected candidate_product_a for pharm_task1 and candidate_product_b for tech_task1, consuming candidates early.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
